confirmed_vulnerabilities.title: Unauthenticated Privilege Escalation via Session Creation (backuply_creating_session)
Unauthenticated Restore Response Handler Leads to Information Disclosure
Temporary Restore PHP File Written to Web Root (backuply-restore.php)
Weak Custom Token Auth in Restore Flow Enables Replay/Brute-force
Database Credentials Passed in HTTP POST Body During Restore

false_positives.reason: PHP_SELF used in preg_replace but only to derive $backup_file_loc path. Not passed to exec/system. The preg_replace uses a safe static pattern (no /e modifier). This is not RCE.
Same as rce-001. $backup_file_loc is a path variable derived from PHP_SELF, used for filesystem path construction, not command execution.
The echo outputs either empty string or literal 'style="display:none"' based on a boolean check (!empty(...)). No user-controlled content is output. The get_option value is only tested for emptiness.
$aws_sse can only be 'checked' or empty string (based on isset check). The output uses esc_attr(). No actual user content reaches output.
$backup_sync_infos can only be 'checked' or empty string. Output uses esc_attr(). Not exploitable.
Output is either 'checked' or empty string based on boolean check of option value. Not XSS.
Same pattern as xss-004. Boolean check on option value, outputs only 'checked' or empty.
Outputs 'display:none' or empty string based on string comparison. No user-controlled data in output.
$history_page is cast to (int). All outputs use esc_html(), esc_attr(), or esc_url(). Properly sanitized. Not exploitable.
$_SERVER['SERVER_SOFTWARE'] is output with esc_html(). Properly escaped. Not exploitable.

backuply_check_backup_status requires backuply_ajax_nonce_verify() which checks nonce + current_user_can('activate_plugins'). Authenticated admin-only. The output goes into JSON (wp_send_json). Low risk.

backuply_get_last_logs requires backuply_ajax_nonce_verify() (nonce + admin capability check). The file_name goes through backuply_sanitize_filename. Log content is read from server files, not user-supplied. Admin-only.
Writes to config file protected by '<?php exit();?>' prefix. Data comes from get_option (admin-controlled). File is chmod 0600. Not exploitable by unauthenticated users.
Data comes from get_option('backuply_remote_backup_locs') which is admin-set. The fopen is used during backup download which requires auth. Not unauthenticated.
Same as file-002. Admin-controlled option data used in backup operations.
$limit is intval() cast. $db and $table come from the backup process internals, not user input. This is the backup DB dump process that runs during authenticated cron/backup operations.
Same as file-004. Internal backup process, not user-controllable.
Same as file-004. Internal backup process.
The restore_curl_url is constructed by the plugin itself (site_url() + known paths). Not user-controllable. The HTTP request is self-referential to trigger async restore. Not SSRF.
The URL is admin_url('admin-ajax.php') + action param. Self-referential loopback request to trigger async backup. Cookie forwarding is intentional for auth. Not SSRF.
Same as ssrf-002. Self-referential async request pattern. URL is admin_url().
License check request goes to hardcoded BACKUPLY_API constant URL. The user-supplied license value goes in the body, not the URL. Not SSRF.
Settings page with nonce_verified=true. Data from admin-only get_option. Requires admin authentication to reach.
Same as file-007. Admin settings page.
Same as file-007. Admin settings page.
Same as file-007. Admin settings page.
Same as file-007. Admin settings page.
Same as file-007. Admin settings page.
Same as file-007. Admin settings page.

backuply_get_last_logs requires backuply_ajax_nonce_verify() which enforces admin auth. File name goes through backuply_sanitize_filename (which calls sanitize_file_name). Path traversal mitigated. Admin-only.
